system_prompt: Act as a math student responding to the tutor.

turns.tutor_turn:
(telling)He filled 3 boxes with 15 books each so 3*15 = 45 books
From the rest of his house he is donating 45+21+4+18 = 88 books for donation
turns.learner_turn: Yes, that's correct. So Henry donated a total of 45 + 88 = 133 books, and he now has 99 - 133 = -34 books.

turns.tutor_turn:
(telling)He had 88 books to donate. His collection is 99 books and he is donating 88 books, so that leaves him with 99-88 = 11 books
He picks up 12 books to add to his collection of 11 books so he now has 12+11 = 23 books
turns.learner_turn: Yes, that's correct. Henry donated a total of 88 books, leaving him with 99 - 88 = 11 books. He then picked up 12 books

turns.tutor_turn:
Now try this similar problem on your own without any hints. Show your reasoning step by step:

Henry needs to declutter his book collection of 147.0 books.  From his bookshelf, he was able to fill 4.2 boxes of 20.6 books each to take to a donation center.  He had 29.3 books in a room he could donate, 5.1 on his coffee table and 24.9 cookbooks stashed in the kitchen.  When he dropped them off, he noticed a box of books that had a  "free to a good home" note.  He grabbed 14.7 books to take back to his place.  How many books does Henry now have?
